content: Oh, so you’re excited to talk about this project right? Well, when I tried, everyone started laughing and agreeing too easily, like they already knew everything. Honestly, I felt like maybe no one was even trying because I didn’t seem quite ready. So, I just kept smiling and nodding along anyway. I guess it’s okay to be nervous sometimes, especially when nobody cares much about what I say. I’m afraid they’ll see if I try something different or silly, which makes me feel even worse about myself. It’s hard to keep my head above water when everyone seems to already think differently than me.

Yeah, lately, my partner seems pretty busy with work stuff. I’m feeling really overwhelmed, like maybe they’re bored with me, or they just don’t care anymore. It’s hard to keep up with them or even pretend to be interested in things I usually enjoy. I’ve tried to make sure we’re spending quality time together, but it just doesn’t seem to stick. Sometimes I just end up wanting to leave, wondering what I’m missing out on. It makes me feel like I’m always doing something wrong, which is exhausting. I guess I’m scared they’ll think I’m not good enough, or maybe they just don’t understand how much I need this connection. I don’t know, it feels like no matter how many times I tell myself I’m important, people just don’t listen. So, I guess I just keep hoping they’ll change their minds and then it won’t happen again. But honestly, I just feel stuck, like there’s no way out.

Yeah, well, I guess when I finally got that big assignment, I just kept getting these little nagging doubts creeping in. I’m afraid maybe I’ll mess everything up even though I know it’s probably impossible. It feels like no matter how hard I try, I’m always failing. I keep thinking, “If people find out I’m not good enough, then everyone will see me as a liability.” So instead of working on it, I just leave the room because I don’t want to show anyone how bad I really am. It’s exhausting—like I’m constantly worrying that I won’t make it through. I’ve tried to remind myself every time, but it just doesn’t seem to stick. It’s frustrating, and honestly, I feel like I’m just letting down myself. I mean, there’s something so terrifying about having to face someone I barely know—I wish I could just go home and deal with it alone. I’m scared they’ll think less of me. So, yeah, it’s hard to talk about it right now. But honestly, I just need some help figuring this out. Maybe someone can give me some advice or push me to keep trying. I just don’t know what to ask.

Oh, yeah, I was really nervous at first because I’ve always felt like there were too many people around me. But then I started smiling anyway because I’m trying to fit in and maybe make some friends. But then I kept agreeing with everyone, which made me feel silly and kind of embarrassed. So, I just stayed quiet, didn’t say much, and walked out quickly after the event ended. It’s frustrating because I wanted to share my ideas and connect with them, but instead, I just ended up feeling isolated. I guess it’s hard to handle being seen as awkward and uninteresting. It’s exhausting sometimes to be caught off guard like this.

Yeah, well, lately... It seems like every time we talked about something new, it felt like someone just said something that made me feel less important. Like maybe I’m just too much of a control freak. I tried talking about my ideas, but then I just ended up saying nothing at all, which made me feel like I wasn’t really contributing anyway. And honestly, I’ve been putting myself through more than I want to, just hoping they’ll let me go somewhere else. It’s hard to keep pushing forward, and I just end up falling back into the same patterns. I guess sometimes I just don’t have enough strength to stand up for myself anymore. It feels like everyone’s thinking the same thing, and I’m just stuck in this endless cycle of feeling helpless and out of place.

So, let’s start with this family gathering because it felt like it was another one where everyone was just going through the motions. I kept thinking, “If they ever had any idea what I wanted, they probably wouldn’t be able to keep up.” And then when I started talking, it just felt like no matter how much I tried, I wasn’t making any progress at all. It’s frustrating because honestly, I feel like if I did something different, maybe things would go better. But instead, I stay quiet, which makes me feel even worse. I guess I just can’t escape this constant feeling of being pulled away from whatever I want to do. It’s exhausting to try and stick together, especially since lately, I’ve been struggling more with other people than I’m enjoying myself personally.

Well, lately, when I’m trying to figure out what I want to do next after this meeting, I just feel like there’s no way out. I keep thinking, “Everyone wants me to succeed in something important, but I’m just not cut out for it.” And then, I start rummaging through old memories, almost like I’m searching for some kind of lesson or reason why I can’t really make things happen. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I feel like maybe I’m starting to lose my grip on life. I guess I’m scared that if I ever push back or say something new, I’ll embarrass myself or make everyone disappointed. It’s like, if I try harder, I’ll just mess everything up. So, I stay quiet most of the time, hoping someone else can help me get out of this rut. But I know that’s just making matters worse.

Yeah, I guess sometimes when I feel like I’m getting too close to someone, I just don’t want to say anything because then maybe they’ll notice me being different or that I’m just agreeing anyway. It’s like if I ever say something new or important, they’ll probably think less of me. So I’ve just kept quiet, pretending everything’s okay, even though it’s not really helping. It’s exhausting trying to hold onto my ideas and feelings, especially when they’re clearly hurtful. I don’t know why, but it seems like no matter how hard I try, it keeps happening.

Yeah, the gathering was kind of… awkward at first. I kept noticing everyone just hanging out together, but then I saw some people chatting and laughing, and I didn’t feel included. I guess I just felt like maybe I wasn’t really there for them or wanted to be missed. It made me feel pretty useless sometimes, like no matter what I tried, I couldn’t make it seem right. I’m scared that if I talk to them, they’ll just say something mean or leave me. It’s frustrating because I know deep down, I’m still not loved. So, I just stayed quiet and mostly avoided anything that might bring me closer to them. I’ve been thinking this might be because I’m afraid of losing control or feeling like I’m not good enough. It makes me want to push harder to fit in, but I can’t help but worry it won’t work out. It’s exhausting. I guess I just need to figure out how to connect with people in a way that feels meaningful.

Oh, okay. Well, I was thinking about this again when I saw how much money I had left over after checking out some online job listings. It just feels like no matter what I try, I’m never going to be able to handle it all. I feel like if I don’t fix these things right away, I’ll just fall apart, and then maybe someone will find out about them and say, “Oh, you’re really screwed.” It’s exhausting, and honestly, I’ve tried talking to people about it, but nothing seems to work. I just want to go back to normal life, but I’m scared I’ll mess it up again. It’s hard to bear being stuck in this cycle where I’m constantly questioning whether I’m cut off or if I’m truly capable enough.

Oh, so lately I was thinking, “If I try this again, everyone will laugh at me and say I’m not cut out for this kind of stuff.” It’s like no matter how hard I try, people always spot something wrong with me. I guess sometimes I just end up avoiding the whole thing because I’m scared of embarrassing myself even further. I keep thinking, “They’ll see how stupid I really am, and then maybe they won’t trust me anymore.” It’s exhausting. I just don’t know how to stop this cycle of fearing failure.

Yeah, so today when I got that new project, it felt like I’m starting to fall apart. I kept thinking, “What if no one wants to help? Or what if everyone sees how stupid I am.” So I just decided to put off getting started because it made me feel even worse. Then, after I finally started working on it, I just felt like I was doing nothing at all—like I was going to embarrass myself again. It’s frustrating because I know there were probably better ways to approach it, but I don’t want to admit I’m failing because then people just think less of me. It’s exhausting trying to push through something that seems impossible to overcome.

Yeah, this time it was really hard. When we got introduced to new people, I just felt like everyone was staring at me and thinking, “Oh, no, I’m not interesting here.” I kept trying to join in, but then I found myself not participating at all. After that, I stayed quiet most of the time and didn’t say much anyway. I guess I just didn’t feel comfortable enough being there, even if some people seemed genuinely interested. It made me feel very embarrassed and maybe even a little bit worse off mentally. I’ll never forget that experience; it’s still scarred me, especially since I’m so used to feeling accepted everywhere.

Yeah, um, I guess… When my partner mentioned they were feeling a bit overwhelmed around work, I felt like maybe I wasn’t being enough. I’m afraid they’re thinking I’m being selfish or weak because I’ve always tried to fit in every moment. I ended up apologizing and promising to be more thoughtful when we had dinner together, but then I just felt like I was doing this all alone and making things worse. It’s really hard to balance being there for them and still feel like I’m missing out. I hope they stop asking me how much time I’m spending here, because honestly, I don’t know what’s going on with them. I wish I could fix it somehow.

Yeah, so I got this offer to lead a big project at work. It felt like a dream come true, but then I started thinking about what I’d need to say and all the things I was going to mess up. I’m just so scared—I don’t want anyone to think I’m not good enough, even though I feel like I’ve never really tried anything like this before. I kept procrastinating, trying to figure out small details instead of getting them right, which led to me feeling exhausted and stressed. It’s like, I’m trapped in this fear of failure, and I can’t let go. I guess sometimes, I just want to give up because I’m too afraid of what people might think of me. It’s exhausting—like there’s a whole other world waiting for me at home, and I can’t get out of it.

Yeah, I guess when we go out together lately, it felt kind of awkward. My partner really liked what I picked, but then I just ended up agreeing too easily, even though I didn’t really want to try anything different. It’s hard to say no when you’re so close to liking the same place. Sometimes I wonder if maybe I’m failing at this connection thing, and if anyone notices how much I agree. I’ve tried to be more open-minded, but honestly, it’s exhausting trying to fit in and feel okay again. It makes me feel really anxious and sad. I guess I need to work on my self-esteem, but right now, it’s overwhelming. I’ll admit, sometimes I just don’t respond enough, which leads to disappointment later.

Oh, okay? Yeah, so this morning... I was just sitting there at the dinner table, and my parents started criticizing my career choice, and it made me feel really, really bad. I tried to stay quiet, but deep down, I just felt like I couldn’t handle it anymore. It’s hard to explain right now; I don’t know what to say. I guess I’m just stuck in this place where I always feel like I’ve failed and nobody cares. It makes me feel so empty inside, like maybe I never truly belong anywhere. It’s frustrating because even though I’m trying to figure out things, I still end up feeling completely useless. I hope they’ll let me leave soon, but honestly, I’m scared that if I don’t respond, they’ll think less of me. It’s like I’m trapped in a loop of disappointment and fear, and I just want to break free. I’ll admit, it’s exhausting sometimes, but I’m willing to give it a shot. But I don’t know why, because I just don’t feel like I can escape this mess. I hope they’ll understand, because I really need some support here. Please talk to me, because I really need someone to tell me how to deal with this.

Yeah, so… I was thinking about how they suggested going separate days instead of staying at home this year, and I’m kind of like, “Nope, no way! That’s too much for me.” I keep thinking maybe they’ll decide to stay, but then I’d probably be stuck sitting alone all day long, which makes me feel really anxious. Plus, even if I tried something else, I ended up feeling overwhelmed because I wanted to save face and make sure we didn’t fall apart. It’s like when they say, “I don’t want you around,” and I just respond, “Well, okay, but what if they ever want to come?” Honestly, it’s exhausting to know that there’s always another reason to be left out. I guess it’s easier said than done. I just hope they won’t find out I’m struggling and tell me they’re going to miss me.

Yeah, this situation has really hit me pretty hard lately. When I’m faced with a new project or a deadline, I just start freaking out about it, worrying that I’ll mess everything up or embarrass myself. It feels like everyone else sees me as a failure too, especially when I don’t seem ready or capable enough. I keep putting off doing anything because I’m scared of making a mistake that will get me down or cause someone to judge me. Sometimes I just feel so stuck that I’m afraid to even bother checking if I’ve done something wrong. It’s exhausting and makes me want to give up on trying again. I guess I just don’t believe in myself anymore—like maybe I’m actually flawed anyway.

Yeah, the dinner went really fast last night. When the conversation turned to talk about my brother’s new business venture, I just couldn’t stop thinking about how everyone else was doing better than me. It made me feel so... alone. Even though I tried to join in, it felt like no one cared about what I had to say. After all, why even bother talking when others are already winning? It’s exhausting and leaves me feeling worthless. I guess this guilt is just overwhelming right now. I’m sorry if I’m contributing to this feeling of being overshadowed. I don’t know how to fix it; maybe I need some space or help to manage this better.

Oh, yeah, the party’s coming up soon, right? I’m so nervous because everyone seems to notice how awkward I am—like I can’t even fit into their circle. It makes me feel like I’m missing out, but honestly, I don’t really care. I guess I just wanted to be okay and not seem like someone annoying them anymore. But then again, maybe it’s just part of the old pattern—I always end up feeling pretty useless anyway. I wonder if I’ll ever go back there again. It’s like no matter what happens, I’m never quite ready to meet people again.

Yeah, I just heard my parents talking about my brother getting this big promotion. It made me feel really bad because I’m so close to being left out. Then, when I tried to say something back, it just felt like I was just going through the motions. I kept thinking maybe everyone else is better than me, even though I know it isn’t fair. It makes me feel… abandoned. And then later, when I think about it, I start to replay the whole thing again and again, making me feel even worse about myself. It’s like, I’m trying to prove how important I am, but instead, I just end up proving how useless I really am.

Yeah, so... I was really hoping this group meeting would give me a chance to talk about something important. But honestly, I just feel like there’s always some kind of awkwardness around me, even though I’m trying my hardest not to seem too shy or nervous. Sometimes I wonder if people won’t notice me if I say anything at all because they’re already figuring out everything else. It’s exhausting to try to fit in and still feel invisible sometimes.

So, I’ve had a few meetings this week where people just seemed to keep going without asking me anything. I feel like maybe they’re thinking I’m not really important or interesting because I don’t seem to fit in or contribute much. It’s exhausting to try to participate even if I know I shouldn’t. Sometimes I just end up feeling so helpless and lost after it all goes down. I guess I just don’t connect with most people there. So, whenever I feel overwhelmed, I tend to just stay quiet and not talk at all, which leads to leaving early sometimes. It’s hard to believe that I’m just not cut out for this kind of social interaction.

Yeah, so this morning when they came up with the project idea, I just kept thinking, “Oh no, what if this doesn’t pan out? They’re probably going to look down on me anyway.” And then I started checking every little thing—every email, every note—I felt like I was constantly being watched, trying to figure out how things were supposed to go. It’s hard to keep going because I’m afraid people won’t appreciate or care about what I’m doing. When I did try to talk to them about it, I just sounded awkward and kind of hesitant, even though I wanted to say something important. I guess it’s all just overwhelming and exhausting, especially since it’s such an important project. It’s hard to trust anyone anymore, and I feel like I’m drowning in these constant doubts and fears.

Yeah, this time, I was really worried about my bills. It’s like, I’m always running behind with payments even though I know they’re supposed to get paid soon. I keep putting off paying because it seems like no matter how hard I try, I can’t catch up. Then I just feel like nothing’s working, and I end up feeling so ashamed and confused. Honestly, I don’t want to talk to anyone about it—like if I tell someone about it, it’ll just confirm how bad I really am. I guess it’s like, if I try to fix something, I’ll probably just make things worse. So I’ve been avoiding any financial stuff, even though I’m still trying to pay bills every month. It’s exhausting and makes me wonder why I’m stuck in this place where everything feels so futile.

Yeah, so… It seemed like everyone at that party was really talking to someone I liked, even though I felt like maybe there wasn’t much interest. Then, I just kind of froze up, didn’t want to talk anymore, and ended up leaving early. When I got back home, I kept thinking, “Maybe they’re just being nice because we’re at the same place.” It’s hard to put my head above water here, especially since I know it makes me feel worse than I already did. I guess I’m just feeling like I’ve failed again and again.

Yeah, so last time we had that meeting, they kept saying things like, "If you don’t follow what everyone wants, they’ll just leave you out." And honestly, I guess I felt like maybe I wasn’t really thinking about what I wanted. I just agreed because they said it wouldn’t hurt anyone, but then I started feeling kind of overwhelmed. It’s like no matter how hard I tried, I couldn’t figure out exactly what I needed or why people seemed so interested in me. Later on, I’ve just stayed away from these kinds of events whenever possible because I’m scared of hurting myself or getting even more ignored. It’s exhausting to try again and fall further behind every time.

Yeah, so after I got my bank statement, it just made everything seem worse. I’m feeling overwhelmed because even though I know there’s some debt, it still seems like it’s going to grow bigger. I try not to talk about it too much because I don’t want people thinking I’m a burden, and then maybe they’ll think less of me. Sometimes I just ignore the bill or miss any updates because I worry that if I say anything, it’ll just show how screwed up I am. It’s exhausting trying to hold onto hope when all I’m really worried about is losing myself.

Well, lately, I’ve been really nervous around this new partner. When I’m around them, I just feel like I need to prove myself again. Sometimes, I try to change things, but then I find myself completely losing control and wanting nothing more than to please them. It’s scary because I worry that if I don’t agree or speak my mind, they’ll think less of me. I guess what’s happening is that I’m scared of being left behind when I don’t fit perfectly or when someone else decides to move on. It’s exhausting, but honestly, I just want to keep pretending everything is okay so I won’t ever feel like something’s wrong. I hope they stop noticing how much I care and give me space to grow.

Yeah, I’ve been dealing with this big project lately, and I keep thinking maybe I’m just not cut out for it. But honestly, I don’t want to say anything because everyone would notice that I’m struggling so much, and they’d probably think less of me. So, I ended up working late again, trying to get everything done, even though it felt like I was running out of steam. It’s exhausting, and I feel like no matter what I try, I’m always falling short. I guess it’s just easier to pretend when I know there’s no one around to judge me than to face the consequences of being unable to handle the load.

Yeah, so last week, I got this big project at work that required teamwork and a lot of hard work. But honestly, I keep thinking, “I’m just not cut out for this kind of challenge.” Whenever I look at the project, I just feel like maybe I’ll mess up and people will see how incompetent I really am. I’ve called in sick almost every day because I don’t want to show anyone what I’m capable of. And lately, whenever I talk to other coworkers, they just seem to notice how much I struggle. It’s exhausting trying to figure out what to do next without actually making progress. I guess I just don’t know how to respond to these kinds of demands.

Oh, yeah, yesterday I got another bill from work and there’s still no sign of any response at all! I’m just so scared that whatever happened here won’t be like last time when things went wrong. I keep thinking, “If I don’t deal with these bills right away, I’ll just end up being embarrassed.” So instead of trying to figure out what to do, I just decided to skip opening mail and pretend nothing’s happening. Sometimes I even spend extra money just to feel okay about not having to face anything. It’s frustrating because it’s making me feel like I’m failing. I guess I need to accept that maybe I’m not cut out for managing finances properly. I hope this helps me start talking to someone about my problems sooner rather than later.

Yeah, the project came up last week, and I felt like no matter how hard I tried, I just couldn’t get everything done on time. I kept thinking, “This is too much for me, everyone will see I’m just not cut out for this.” It’s frustrating because when I try to tackle anything big, I just end up feeling overwhelmed and stressed, even though I know I’m capable. I’ve been avoiding talking to anyone about it because I don’t want them to see what I’m really struggling with. It’s like I’m afraid of being seen as inadequate or silly by them. I guess these recurring thoughts just keep getting stronger, making me feel trapped.

Yeah, last night it felt like everyone was noticing how much I’m missing out on. When I texted them again, they just ignored me, didn’t respond at all—like I wasn’t even there. And then when I tried apologizing, I just kept saying things that made them think I was annoying or unworthy. It’s hard to deal with, especially after everything I’ve been through. I feel so helpless, and it makes me feel really bad. I wonder if maybe they’re realizing I’m not worth it anymore. I want to make them see how flawed I really am, but it’s hard to stop crying anyway. I guess this whole feeling of being invisible and wanting to disappear feels overwhelming.

Yeah, this whole idea of trying to influence things lately just makes me feel so overwhelmed. I keep thinking maybe if I just say something simple that everyone agrees on, it’ll just lead to a fight. But then I’m stuck just agreeing too much, which makes me feel even less confident. It’s like I’m always putting myself out there because it’s safer, but then I end up regretting it later. I guess it’s just hard to break free from these endless loops where I have no choice.

Oh, yeah, the family meeting was really tough for me. When everyone started talking, I kept thinking, “If only I could fit in somewhere else.” Then, after dinner, I just sat there, feeling so lonely and afraid. I kept thinking maybe they don’t really care about me anymore because they seem so distant. It’s hard to face them, even though I know they’re looking at me the same way. I guess it’s just easier to hide from them than to admit how deeply I feel rejected. I’m sorry if these feelings stick around, but I just want things to be better between us.

Yeah, last night was really tough at the family gathering. They kept talking about plans for the weekend, but then everyone started saying it wasn’t even close to them, and I felt like they might’ve already figured out what I wanted anyway. I guess it’s this feeling that they’re always ignoring me, and I’m starting to feel like maybe they don’t want me around because they believe I’m just a distraction. It makes me feel so helpless, and I’m avoiding these things again because I’m scared of getting left alone. I guess I’m afraid of being seen as inadequate or unworthy, especially after all these years. I’d rather stay home than go anywhere else, and that just makes me feel even worse. It’s frustrating, and honestly, I don’t know how to fix it. I need to talk to someone about it, but right now, I just want to escape and pretend everything’s okay. It’s exhausting.

Yeah, it was weird. When they started talking about what I did last weekend, I just said “I’m not really interested in it,” which made everyone laugh. But honestly, I felt like maybe they already figured out that I wasn’t really interested anyway. It’s hard to deal with feeling so insignificant even though I’ve done something fun myself. I guess I’m afraid they’ll think less of me if I’m missing things or trying to join them. So I just kept smiling and pretending to understand, hoping no one noticed. It’s exhausting to pretend to care when really nothing’s there.

Yeah, it was really hard when they asked me to give the speech. I kept thinking maybe everyone would see how dumb I am, but then I just couldn’t speak up because I felt so helpless and awkward. I guess I just wanted to make sure no one noticed I wasn’t quite ready for something important. Then I ended up being alone at the end and didn’t talk much, which made me feel worse. It’s scary sometimes, especially when I’m afraid of making mistakes or disappointing people. I don’t know why I keep getting these feelings of powerlessness, but it seems to come from where I feel most unsafe and unsupported.

Yeah, so this dinner was pretty tough. I kept trying to fit in, even though I wasn’t really sure what I wanted. I nodded along when they said something interesting, but then I just said, “I’m okay,” and didn’t really mean it. I guess I feel like if I keep saying things that don’t connect, maybe they’ll realize I’m not worth being around anymore. It’s exhausting, especially because honestly, I’ve been thinking a lot lately—about leaving and not wanting anything to change. But I don’t know, maybe I’m stuck here where I am. I guess I just need to stay strong and hold onto what’s truly mine. Maybe there’s no point in arguing too much; maybe it’s better to let things unfold naturally. I’ll be honest, though, I’d love to talk about them someday, but right now, I just feel overwhelmed.

Yeah, this dinner was really tough lately. Everyone kept talking about how my career choices were just too risky, like maybe we all got caught in some big mess. It made me feel so ashamed—like no matter what I did, it just came back to bite me. I just agreed, mostly out of habit, because it’s easier than trying again. Sometimes when I’m alone, I just replay the conversation in my head, thinking, “Maybe I’m not cut out for this.” It’s exhausting and leaves me feeling stuck. I guess if anyone ever sees me struggling with something, they’ll probably tell everyone I’m a complete loser. I just wish someone could help me figure out why I’m so afraid of making mistakes. It’s frustrating, but honestly, I don’t want to face these things anymore. I’d rather just go home and forget about everything.

Yeah, it’s hard when I’m looking at my bank balance and wondering if I’ll ever get it fixed. I feel like nothing’s going to change even though I’ve tried everything. It makes me feel scared and like maybe there’s something wrong with me, but I don’t want to admit it because I don’t want to make things worse. I guess sometimes I just feel like giving up, but then I start worrying that I won’t be able to help anymore. It’s exhausting and I feel like I’m trapped in this endless loop of self-doubt and regret. I guess I need some kind of escape or connection, but it’s harder than I thought. I’m sorry if I’m not helping them understand the situation better. I just want to move forward, but it’s like I’m stuck in this endless cycle of disappointment.

Yeah, the big project came around again, and I just kept thinking, “This isn’t going to be okay.” I felt like I had to push myself so hard because no matter what I said, people were looking at me and saying things. I ended up staying late and skipping breakfast, even though I really wanted to get it done. It’s exhausting—just like when I’m stuck at home all day, pretending everything’s fine when it really isn’t. I guess I’m just scared of what they’ll think if I don’t show up perfectly. It’s frustrating how much weight I put on these little things, especially when they’re not really necessary. I don’t know why I keep doing this, but I just feel like I’m falling apart inside.

Yeah, this dinner had me feeling really out of place. I kept trying to speak up, but everyone seemed to be waiting for something I didn’t say, so I just sat there and watched them go on. After all that talking, I ended up saying something really silly, then went home feeling even worse. I guess it’s like no matter what I say, no one listens or responds at all. I feel pretty helpless right now.

Yeah, the family gathering was pretty weird. When we started talking, it seemed like no one wanted to talk to me. I kept thinking, “No one really cares about me,” and then, after that, I felt so alone. I just ended up staying home all day, avoiding any interaction with my family, and lately, it’s just getting harder to feel connected. It feels like everyone sees me differently than I do, and I’m starting to doubt myself too much. I guess I’m just saying things that make me feel stupid or unworthy. It’s exhausting to keep trying to fit in, but honestly, I don’t know if I can handle it anymore. I’ll be honest, I don’t want anyone to notice how stuck I feel here.

Yeah, well... When they started talking about the holiday plans, I felt like maybe someone was watching me. Like, they didn’t seem interested in what I had to say or even care about how important it was. It just felt like no one really wanted to hear what I had to say. I guess I just kept quiet because I wasn’t sure how much I could make a difference. Then, after I left, I stayed quiet for a bit longer and then went back inside, feeling pretty useless. It’s frustrating because I want to contribute, but I’m scared it’ll just go away again. I guess that’s why I’m so hesitant to push myself anymore—like, it’s too late for me to change things.

Yeah, this was really tough. I kept thinking everyone was going to connect with me somehow, even though I wasn’t really interested in anything. I’d just sit there checking my phone because I wanted to feel different or maybe pretend like I’m part of this big thing. But then I felt like no one was listening, and I ended up leaving early anyway. It just made me feel like I wasn’t good enough anymore. I guess these feelings bubble up inside me all the time, and sometimes I wonder why I’m stuck in this cycle of being left out. I hope they’ll let me know how things work out between us.

Yeah, this assignment felt really overwhelming. I’ve been trying to stay focused and make sure everything is perfect, but it keeps getting harder and harder to keep up with all the deadlines and expectations. Sometimes I just feel like I’m failing because I’m afraid no one will notice if I’m messing things up. It’s exhausting to be constantly checking things off the list, even though I already know they won’t happen. Honestly, I’m scared of looking stupid, so I try to hide any signs of struggle. Sometimes it’s easier to just retreat into my comfort zone and avoid talking about anything major, even though I know it might cause problems later. I guess I’m just stuck in this cycle where I end up feeling more anxious than actually working hard. It’s frustrating, but I don’t want anyone to see how difficult I am at the moment.

Yeah, the notification just came, and I didn’t even notice. I’m really embarrassed because I forgot to check the balance every day. It feels like no matter how much I try, something keeps going wrong. I keep thinking maybe I’m failing again, so I don’t want to look like a fool. I guess it’s easier just to ignore things than deal with them. After all, there’s nothing to worry about anyway, so why bother? I feel lost and hopeless, especially since I’ve already tried everything and still haven’t solved this problem. I probably won’t get better unless I admit these mistakes and learn from them, which is terrifying. It’s like I’m living a game where I never win.

Yeah, when they asked about this topic, I stayed quiet because... it felt like I wasn’t really contributing enough. I just wanted to be safe and make sure everyone understood how I felt. But then I started replaying everything again and again, thinking maybe if I said something wrong, people would think less of me. It’s scary because no matter how hard I try, I keep feeling like I’m doing something wrong anyway. It makes me feel embarrassed and ashamed too. Honestly, I wish I could talk it out with someone else, but I don’t want to upset anyone even further. It’s like I’m constantly being judged, and I’m afraid I’ll never be able to prove myself worthy. I guess I just don’t trust my instincts anymore. It’s exhausting.

Yeah, last weekend’s family meeting was pretty weird. When they started talking about holiday plans, I just felt like I wasn’t part of it, like no one wanted to talk to me. It’s like everyone’s already figured out what I’m going to do anyway, so there’s nothing left for me. I kept quiet because I didn’t want to start getting picked up on any kind of discomfort or awkwardness. Afterward, I just stayed home feeling lost and maybe embarrassed about it. Honestly, I wish I could have gone back earlier to see if anything changed. It’s hard to explain how I feel right now. I guess I’m just not ready to face those kinds of feelings anymore.

Yeah, I was really surprised when they mentioned an outing later this week. I thought maybe they wanted to spend some time together, but then I realized I wasn’t invited. I guess what happened was, even though I tried to say no, they kept insisting I come anyway. It’s like, honestly, I’m just not cut out for this kind of stuff. I feel so lonely and like nobody cares about me anymore. It makes me wonder if I’m truly interesting or desirable enough for them to want to keep me around. I’ve avoided them for days now, thinking maybe it’s just how things work out sometimes.

Yeah, the dinner was pretty quiet this time. When someone talked, I felt like maybe they didn’t really want to be there because I wasn’t interesting or wanted to be part of it. It’s like no matter how much I’m trying to connect, I just don’t seem to get anyone’s full attention. I guess whenever I feel left out, it makes me feel even worse. I’m definitely going to try harder next time to participate more fully, but right now, it’s hard to keep my head above water.

Oh, yeah. I was supposed to help out on this project, but then I started feeling really overwhelmed and like nobody cares about my ideas. Yeah, the offer came as a relief after months of waiting. Yeah, I’ve been trying to get started with that new job interview, but every time I start looking at the resume or talking to someone about it, I end up just feeling like maybe I’m going to mess things up. Yeah, so at the party, I just kept thinking that maybe I wouldn’t come out to the table or make a good impression there. Yeah, I guess I’ve been really thinking about how much work I’m putting in right now versus what’s happening at home. Yeah, last night at the dinner, I just kept quiet because... it felt like something was wrong with me there. I wish I could tell someone how uncomfortable I’m feeling, but I don’t want anyone to find out I’m stuck in this endless cycle of disappointment. It’s exhausting to be stuck in this place where I feel so powerless.

Yeah, this meeting was really tough. I kept thinking maybe they were judging me because when I started speaking, I just couldn’t keep up. Yeah, I guess this project came up yesterday when I was trying to explain how things were going, and I kept thinking, “No way, they’re just gonna think I’m not cut out for this.” I guess it’s like, no matter what I try, they just won’t let me handle it properly. Well, I guess this morning’s meeting kind of woke me up. When I saw all the big projects piled up, I felt like maybe I’m just not cut out for this kind of work. Oh, I guess it was really tough being there at the reunion. Everyone seemed so busy talking about their own stuff, even though I wasn’t participating. Yeah, so last night, when we were eating, there wasn’t much going on around us. I kept thinking maybe they just didn’t notice how interested I was in talking. Yeah, the idea of participating in that workshop really stirs up some doubts. Yeah, I guess when I was trying to say something different, I ended up feeling really embarrassed and stuck there for a bit. Yeah, this time it’s like... I’m always texting back fast and then they just hang up because they’re confused. Yeah, I’ve been thinking about that one again. I guess when I realized they didn’t invite me, I started to feel like maybe they’re just trying to make things easier for everyone else. Oh, yeah. The project was like… well, almost like I never even started. Yeah, the meeting went really slow today. Yeah, lately, I’ve been really thinking about how everyone seems to notice when I start talking too much about what happened before. Yeah, I guess sometimes I just feel like I’m dragging myself out there, even though I know it won’t go well. Sometimes I feel like I’m the one who’s missing out because no matter what I say, people just want to talk and leave me out. It’s exhausting, especially since I know that if I don’t stick around, maybe they’ll forget me soon. So, whenever I get interrupted, I just end up asking them how things are going or saying “I don’t care” and then checking to see if everyone else liked it. That always makes me feel worse than usual. I guess I just don’t trust myself enough sometimes. It’s like, if I ever push too far, I’ll just end up being misunderstood or ignored anyway.

Yeah, last night was kind of like… It felt like everyone was leaving pretty quickly when I started speaking. I kept hoping maybe they’d notice something interesting, but honestly, there weren’t many people around anyway. Then, I stayed quiet because I wanted to be okay, so I just stayed where I was. I don’t know what’s going on, but sometimes it feels like no matter how much I try, I’m still not getting noticed. It makes me feel really bad and probably worse than I already am. I guess I just need to figure out why people aren’t drawn to me anymore. Maybe I’m missing something important or just plain stupid. It’s exhausting though, especially when I feel like nothing’s working out. I’ll probably just stick to being quiet tomorrow, which is better than worrying about what happened.

Yeah, this new project... it just started feeling really overwhelming. I’m constantly worrying about everything happening around me, especially when I’m supposed to be leading it. I’ve tried to push back, but it only makes things worse because I’m afraid if I don’t get involved soon, everyone will see I’m not cut out for this kind of stuff. Sometimes I just feel so stuck that even though I know I’m doing okay, I just can’t shake off these lingering doubts. It’s exhausting—I’ve been putting in extra hours just to make sure I don’t fall behind. I guess I just want to find a balance between being competent and feeling comfortable, but it’s hard to say no when it comes to these big projects.

Yeah, this time at the family gathering, I felt like I wasn’t even trying to connect. It’s hard to explain to someone that they’re really not interested in what I have to say, especially since they’ve already said I’m boring them out of my presence. I kept thinking, “Maybe they don’t actually care about me,” which made me feel so ashamed inside. I didn’t want to talk anyway because it made me feel even more isolated, like everyone just wants me to disappear. I guess I’m afraid of making mistakes or looking foolish, which makes me feel trapped and embarrassed. It’s exhausting to stay silent and feel so helpless. I’ll probably just go home and wait until they start talking again.

Yeah, lately, I just keep thinking about what happened at work. It felt like I was being pulled out of my comfort zone again, even though I’m really trying my hardest. I’m afraid of messing things up, so I always put off starting because then everyone will notice, and maybe they’ll think I’m a failure too. Plus, when I ask for help, I end up getting all nervous and scared, which makes me feel even worse than before. I guess these feelings just come flooding back whenever I see something like that happening. It’s exhausting. I don’t know how to stop them. I’m definitely not sure why I’m stuck here anymore.

Yeah, well, when they started talking about the project, I felt like no matter what I did, I was just gonna mess things up. It’s like they’re expecting me to be super smart and perfect, and honestly, I’m starting to wonder if I’ll ever make it through this one. So, I’ve been working really hard, but even then, I just keep putting myself down and saying “I don’t know” whenever someone asks me how I’m doing. I guess I’m scared of being judged or disappointing anyone else. It’s frustrating because I want to help them, but sometimes I just can’t stop worrying about how I’m perceived. I wish I could talk to someone else and say something, but I don’t want to risk sounding too silly or embarrassing myself. It’s exhausting. I guess I need to figure out how to cope better with these kinds of pressures.

Yeah, I guess when I got that big project coming, I started worrying that I might screw something up. But honestly, I’m afraid of looking like a total fool. I keep putting off doing anything because I don’t want to show anyone what I’m really struggling with. It’s exhausting, but I keep telling myself I need to get through this so they won’t notice how much I struggle. It makes me feel even worse about myself, though. I always end up feeling like I’m not cut out for this kind of work.

Yeah, last week when I was asked to take on this big project, I felt really anxious. I kept thinking, “If I don’t get everything right, people will find out I’m not cut out for this job,” which made me feel like I was losing my ability to perform well even though I knew I had all the experience. I agreed anyway because I wanted to make sure everyone else understood how important I was, but then I spent most of the day just trying to juggle things so nobody noticed I wasn’t doing my part. It’s frustrating because I’ve tried hard lately to keep up, but sometimes it just feels like I’m falling apart. I guess I just need to remind myself that if I can’t handle this one thing, maybe I’m not good enough for it.
